title: astragalus labradoricus var. occidentalis
intro: Astragalus labradoricus var. occidentalis, also known as Western Milk Vetch, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stress, promoting overall health and well-being. The medicinal properties of Western Milk Vetch include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-boosting effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fatigue, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Western Milk Vetch can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
